'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Is a descendant of Watatsumi.]]<!-- Okitsu Yoso. https://shinto.miraheze.org/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Generations_of_Watatsumi {{ill|Okitsu Yoso|...|ja|JAPANESE|...}} ==Output Template== Output should take a form like the following '''{{ill|Okitsu Yoso|...|ja|JAPANESE|...}}''' (JAPANESE__NOINDEX__} is a descendant of Watatsumi. __NOINDEX__ [[Category:is a descendant of Watatsumi. --> [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Is a descendant of Watatsumi.]] [[Category:Dd]][[ja:瀛津世襲命]] ]]

'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <!-- Okitsu Yoso. https://shinto.miraheze.org/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Generations_of_Watatsumi {{ill|Okitsu Yoso|...|ja|JAPANESE|...}} ==Output Template== Output should take a form like the following '''{{ill|Okitsu Yoso|...|ja|JAPANESE|...}}''' (JAPANESE__NOINDEX__} is a descendant of Watatsumi. __NOINDEX__ [[Category:is a descendant of Watatsumi. --> [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]
[[ja:瀛津世襲命]] ]]

According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>

His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>

revision.text:
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm?utm_source=chatgpt.com</ref>
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== <!-- Okitsu Yoso. https://shinto.miraheze.org/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Generations_of_Watatsumi {{ill|Okitsu Yoso|...|ja|JAPANESE|...}} ==Output Template== Output should take a form like the following '''{{ill|Okitsu Yoso|...|ja|JAPANESE|...}}''' (JAPANESE__NOINDEX__} is a descendant of Watatsumi. __NOINDEX__ [[Category:is a descendant of Watatsumi. --> [[Category:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okitsu Yoso-qq]] [[Category:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Okitsu Yoso-qq]] [[Category:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okitsu Yoso]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:瀛津世襲命]] ]]

According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

revision.text:
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>

Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

revision.text:
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

revision.text:
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Tier 2]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: mark ja import issue
revision.text:
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Ja import failed]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

revision.text:
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.

His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>

'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Ja import failed]] to [[Category:pages with link uncertainty]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===
{{draft categories| [[Category:is a descendant of Watatsumi. -->
[[Category:Completion tier 2]]
}}

Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.

He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀛津世襲]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀛津世襲]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀛津世襲]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:瀛津世襲]]
'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.
== From Japanese ==
Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto (瀛津世襲命) is a legendary figure in ancient Japanese tradition, regarded as the ancestor of the Owari clan. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'' (Chronicles of Japan) and ''Kojiki'' (Records of Ancient Matters), he is the son of Amanootoko-no-Mikoto (天忍男命). His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto (世襲足媛命), became the empress of Emperor Kōshō. Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is said to have served as a minister (''Ōomi'', 大臣) during Emperor Kōshō's reign and is considered the progenitor of the Owari Muraji clan.<ref>https://rekichi.net/s/22058163</ref>
In the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (Ancient Chronicles of Former Generations), he is also referred to as Katsuragi Hiko-no-Mikoto (葛木彦命) and described as serving as a minister during Emperor Kōshō’s period, contributing to the imperial court.<ref>https://sendaikujihongi.seisaku.bz/m05.html</ref>
Additionally, the family of Ōkitsu Yosonomikoto is closely associated with the Owari clan, with his descendants believed to be the ancestors of the Owari Muraji lineage. His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>

'''Okitsu Yoso''' is a figure in Japanese mythology, recognized as the ancestor of the Owari clan. He is mentioned in ancient Japanese chronicles, including the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki. His sister, Yosotarashi-hime, was the empress of Emperor Kōshō, the fifth emperor of Japan.<ref>https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/okitsuyoso/</ref><ref>https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2634.html</ref>
The Owari clan, descending from Okitsu Yoso, was a prominent family in ancient Japan. The Nihon Shoki refers to Okitsu Yoso as the ancestor of the Ohari no Muraji, indicating his significant role in the lineage of this clan.<ref>https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Nihongi%3A_Chronicles_of_Japan_from_the_Earliest_Times_to_A.D._697/Book_IV</ref>
While specific details about Okitsu Yoso's life and deeds are limited, his mention in these early texts underscores his importance in the genealogies of noble families in Japan's early history.

His sister, Yosotarashi Hime-no-Mikoto, becoming the empress of Emperor Kōshō further solidified the family’s influence within the court. <ref>https://miko.org/~uraki/kuon/furu/text/siryaku/siryaku01-002.htm</ref>
